growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h computed as ln(2)/doubling_time_h. Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.medium: modified Allen mineral base medium + 0.2% w/v tryptone
growth_records.cite: Andersson et al. Global analysis of mRNA stability in the archaeon Sulfolobus. Genome Biology (2006). DOI:10.1186/gb-2006-7-10-r99. [PMC1794556]
growth_records.snippet: S. solfataricus DSM1617 and S. acidocaldarius DSM639 ... cultures were grown at 79°C in modified Allen mineral base medium containing 0.2% w/v tryptone... doubling times of 3.5 and 6 h were obtained for S. acidocaldarius
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (3.5 h) reported directly in Materials and Methods (exponential-phase batch culture, OD600 monitoring). growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu = ln(2)/td = ln(2)/3.5 = 0.198 h^-1. Oxygen status inferred as aerobic (standard for Sulfolobus grown in shaken Erlenmeyer flasks); pH not stated in text.

growth_records.lvlwhy: the source paper (often a review/modeling reuse) states no strain
growth_records.cite: Barillà et al. Driving Apart and Segregating Genomes in Archaea. Trends in Microbiology (2016). DOI:10.1016/j.tim.2016.07.001. [PMC5120986]
growth_records.snippet: Sulfolobus acidocaldarius (doubling time ∼213 minutes), are characterized by a brief G1 prereplication period that accounts for no more than 5% of the entire cell cycle
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (~213 min = 3.55 h) reported in this review. growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu = ln2/doubling_time_h = 0.6931/3.55 = 0.1953 /h. Medium/temperature/pH not stated in this review text, left null.

growth_records.medium: Brock's Medium (0.1% tryptone, 0.2% sucrose, 10 ug/ml uracil)
growth_records.cite: Samson, Rachel Y. et al. Coordination of chromosome segregation and cell division in the archaeon Sulfolobus acidocaldarius. Nature Communications (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-67934-8. [PMC12858884]
growth_records.snippet: The mutant cells grew with a reduced rate (generation time 5.6 +/- 0.06 h) compared to wild-type (generation time 4.8 +/- 0.06 h). All S. acidocaldarius strains were grown in Brock's Medium containing 0.1% tryptone and 0.2% sucrose ... supp
growth_records.notes: Sulfolobus acidocaldarius is an archaeon (prokaryote). Only measured growth physiology in this cell-biology paper is bulk generation (doubling) time; NO substrate uptake, product secretion, or MFA fluxes are reported. Reported doubling time td = 4.8 +/- 0.06 h (wild-type, from OD600 growth curves, Fig. 3a). Specific growth rate CALCULATED as mu = ln2/td = 0.693147/4.8 = 0.14441 /h. Growth at 78 C (Methods 'Growth conditions'; also 75 C used for live-cell imaging). Medium pH not stated numerically; text notes the Sulfolobus growth medium is below the pKa of acetic acid (4.76) and intracellular pH is 6-6.5, so medium pH < 4.76 (acidic Brock medium) but exact value not given -> pH left null.

growth_records.strain: delta-segAB deletion mutant (derived from SK-1 background)
growth_records.medium: Brock's Medium (0.1% tryptone, 0.2% sucrose, 10 ug/ml uracil)
growth_records.cite: Samson, Rachel Y. et al. Coordination of chromosome segregation and cell division in the archaeon Sulfolobus acidocaldarius. Nature Communications (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-67934-8. [PMC12858884]
growth_records.snippet: The mutant cells grew with a reduced rate (generation time 5.6 +/- 0.06 h) compared to wild-type (generation time 4.8 +/- 0.06 h) (Fig. 3a).
growth_records.notes: Genetic delta-segAB knockout strain (not wild-type physiology). Reported doubling time td = 5.6 +/- 0.06 h from OD600 growth curves (Fig. 3a). Specific growth rate CALCULATED as mu = ln2/td = 0.693147/5.6 = 0.12378 /h. Same medium/temperature as wild-type record. No uptake/secretion/MFA data reported. Included for completeness; mutant strain is less useful for GEM constraint than the wild-type record.

growth_records.medium: Vienna Defined (VD) Medium, modified carbon sources

up.method: HPLC (Aminex HPX-87H) of supernatant; qGlc, values in figures only
photometric glutamate assay (Cedex Bio HT); qMSG, values in figures only

sec.method: HPLC (Aminex HPX-87H); qTre, values in figures only

yields.note: OD600-to-DCW correlation factor 0.586 g/L per OD600; C-balance closed at 1.0 +/- 0.1

growth_records.cite: Rastädter et al. Physiological Characterization of Sulfolobus acidocaldarius in a Controlled Bioreactor Environment. Int J Environ Res Public Health (2021). DOI:10.3390/ijerph18115532. [PMC8196767]
growth_records.snippet: Based on the exponential increase of the CO2 concentration in the exhaust gas during the batch phase at the beginning of the cultivation, muMax was determined as 0.097 h-1.
growth_records.notes: muMax=0.097 /h reported (batch, exhaust CO2). Doubling time = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.097 = 7.15 h. Full VD salt composition given in ref [22] (Quehenberger et al.), not restated here; only modified C-source concentrations listed. Specific q values plotted in Figs 2/5/6, not tabulated.

growth_records.medium: Vienna Defined (VD) Medium, modified carbon sources

up.method: HPLC; qGlc, figure only
glutamate assay; qMSG, figure only
exhaust gas O2, qO2, figure only

yields.note: C-balance ~1.0; RQ (CO2/O2) approximately constant across pH; highest cell density of the pH series at pH 3.0

growth_records.cite: Rastädter et al. Physiological Characterization of Sulfolobus acidocaldarius in a Controlled Bioreactor Environment. Int J Environ Res Public Health (2021). DOI:10.3390/ijerph18115532. [PMC8196767]
growth_records.snippet: Reproducibility experiments, in which three reactors were subjected to the same conditions (75 C, pH 3.0, D = 0.03 h-1), showed a standard deviation <3% for OD, mu and qMSG, whereas qGlc and qTre exhibited a STD of <10%.
growth_records.notes: Steady-state chemostat: mu = D = 0.03 /h. Doubling time = 0.693/0.03 = 23.1 h. pH 3.0 identified as optimum (highest cell density). qGlc, qMSG, qTre, qO2, qCO2 measured but numeric values only in Figs 2/3.

growth_records.medium: Vienna Defined (VD) Medium, modified carbon sources

up.method: HPLC; qGlc decreased considerably at pH 2.0 (figure only)
glutamate assay; qMSG slightly (non-significantly) higher at pH 2.0

sec.method: exhaust gas; CO2 yield elevated at pH 2.0

yields.note: CO2 yield increased at pH 2.0 vs other pH; cell density 27% lower than pH 3.0

growth_records.cite: Rastädter et al. Physiological Characterization of Sulfolobus acidocaldarius in a Controlled Bioreactor Environment. Int J Environ Res Public Health (2021). DOI:10.3390/ijerph18115532. [PMC8196767]
growth_records.snippet: The specific glucose uptake rate, qGlc, decreased considerably at pH 2.0... Solely the CO2 yield increased at pH 2.0 compared to the other pH conditions.
growth_records.notes: Steady-state chemostat mu=D=0.03/h; doubling 23.1 h. Distinct reactor (2 L Biostat, different aeration/stirring) because DASbox pH2.0 run gave C-balance >1.0 and was excluded. Cell density 27% below pH 3.0.

growth_records.medium: Vienna Defined (VD) Medium, modified carbon sources

up.method: HPLC; qGlc (figure only)
glutamate assay; qMSG increased with D (figure only)
exhaust O2; qO2 (figure only)

sec.method: HPLC; qTre lowest at low D (figure only)

yields.note: Lowest RQ of the D-series at D=0.010

growth_records.cite: Rastädter et al. Physiological Characterization of Sulfolobus acidocaldarius in a Controlled Bioreactor Environment. Int J Environ Res Public Health (2021). DOI:10.3390/ijerph18115532. [PMC8196767]
growth_records.snippet: The highest RQ of 1.18 was determined at D = 0.064 h-1 and the lowest at D = 0.010 h-1.
growth_records.notes: Steady-state chemostat mu=D=0.010/h; doubling=0.693/0.010=69.3 h. Part of D-series 0.010-0.097 /h at pH 3.0. q values figure-only.

growth_records.medium: Vienna Defined (VD) Medium, modified carbon sources

up.method: HPLC; qGlc (figure only)
glutamate assay; qMSG (figure only)

growth_records.cite: Rastädter et al. Physiological Characterization of Sulfolobus acidocaldarius in a Controlled Bioreactor Environment. Int J Environ Res Public Health (2021). DOI:10.3390/ijerph18115532. [PMC8196767]
growth_records.snippet: S. acidocaldarius cultures were subjected to different dilution rates ranging from 0.010 to 0.097 h-1 at a constant pH of 3.0.
growth_records.notes: Steady-state chemostat mu=D=0.021/h; doubling=0.693/0.021=33.0 h. q values figure-only.

growth_records.medium: Vienna Defined (VD) Medium, modified carbon sources

up.method: HPLC; qGlc (figure only); glucose began to accumulate at D=0.042
glutamate assay; qMSG (figure only); MSG accumulated only from D=0.052

growth_records.cite: Rastädter et al. Physiological Characterization of Sulfolobus acidocaldarius in a Controlled Bioreactor Environment. Int J Environ Res Public Health (2021). DOI:10.3390/ijerph18115532. [PMC8196767]
growth_records.snippet: The cell density was the highest at a dilution rate of 0.032 h-1.
growth_records.notes: Steady-state chemostat mu=D=0.032/h; doubling=0.693/0.032=21.7 h. Highest cell density of D-series. q values figure-only.

growth_records.medium: Vienna Defined (VD) Medium, modified carbon sources

up.method: HPLC; qGlc (figure only); residual glucose accumulating past D=0.042
glutamate assay; qMSG (figure only)

yields.note: D-glucose starts to accumulate at D=0.042; L-glutamate still fully consumed

growth_records.cite: Rastädter et al. Physiological Characterization of Sulfolobus acidocaldarius in a Controlled Bioreactor Environment. Int J Environ Res Public Health (2021). DOI:10.3390/ijerph18115532. [PMC8196767]
growth_records.snippet: A dilution rate of 0.074 h-1 led to a rapid decrease of biomass concentration in the reactor. Washout... occurred between 0.084 and 0.097 h-1.
growth_records.notes: mu=D=0.074/h (marginal, biomass declining, near-washout); doubling=0.693/0.074=9.4 h. Fitted muMax: 0.077/h (MSG) and 0.095/h (glucose) via Monod; batch muMax 0.097/h. Ks glucose 0.99 g/L, Ks MSG 0.15 g/L (6.6x higher glutamate affinity). Dcrit ~0.077-0.078/h.

growth_records.cite: Rastadter, Kerstin et al. kLa based scale-up cultivation of the extremophilic archaeon Sulfolobus acidocaldarius: from benchtop to pilot scale. Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2023). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2023.1160012. [PMC10441222]
growth_records.snippet: a dilution rate of 0.03 h-1 was set via the feed rate and kept constant... The measured DCW slightly increased from 3.77 to 4.16 g/L during the scale-up... The growth rate, mu, the specific uptake rates of the two carbon sources, qMSG and q
growth_records.notes: 2 L chemostat, steady state, D=0.03 h-1 so mu=D=0.03 h-1 (growth_method calculated: mu=D at steady state; also measured from DCW difference Eq.2). doubling_time=ln2/0.03=23.1 h. DCW=3.77 g/L (2 L). Biomass space-time yield D*x=0.03*3.77=0.113 g/L/h. Specific rates qGlc, qMSG, qTre, qO2, qCO2 were experimentally determined (HPLC + off-gas + Eqs.4,5,11,12) but numeric values are reported only in Figure 2C/2E, not in text/tables, so rates left null with is_experimental true. Full VD mineral medium composition given in Quehenberger et al. 2019 (cited); only modified C-source concentrations stated here. Viability ~91% (FCM).

up.method: HPLC; qGlc via steady-state balance (Eq.4), Figure 2C

growth_records.cite: Rastadter, Kerstin et al. kLa based scale-up cultivation of the extremophilic archaeon Sulfolobus acidocaldarius: from benchtop to pilot scale. Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2023). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2023.1160012. [PMC10441222]
growth_records.snippet: Sulfolobus acidocaldarius (DSM 639) | 200 | Continuous | Glutamate and glucose | 0.125 | d = 0.03 h-1 | 4.16 ... in contrast to 4.16 g/L observed in this study
growth_records.notes: 200 L pilot chemostat, steady state, D=0.03 h-1 so mu=D=0.03 h-1. doubling_time=ln2/0.03=23.1 h. Final DCW=4.16 g/L (Table 1). Biomass space-time yield D*x=0.03*4.16=0.125 g/L/h (Table 1). qGlc/qMSG/qTre comparable to smaller scales (Figure 2C); qO2/qCO2/RQ not calculable due to faulty off-gas measurement at 200 L. Full VD mineral salts in Quehenberger et al. 2019 (cited).
